text: The surgeon identifies the transition point between the collapsed and dilated loops of the small bowel. The pale mucosa of the collapsed bowel contrasts with the hyperemic red mucosa of the dilated bowel. A previously divided band is visible, clearly marking the transition point.

The surgeon observes the viable bowel on both sides of the constriction ring, indicating a positive sign. The video shows the proximal bowel with a thickened and scarred mesentery near the SMA and SMV pedicle, around which the bowel is twisted. The scarred area is unraveled, revealing the twisted bowel. Further adhesions are carefully removed to prevent future complications.

The surgeon locates the ileocolic pedicle after releasing the obstruction and unraveling any twists. They then proceed to run the entire length of the small bowel from the ileocolic pedicle to the duodenojejunal (DJ) flexure.
